content.claim: Hungary's early academic selection via 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks creates a three-stage selection funnel, with students able to exit basic education and enter selective secondary schools at ages 10, 12, and 14, in addition to selection at school entry at age 7.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Describes institutional structure but does not quantify participation rates or selection criteria variation
Does not address implementation consistency across regions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
